desc:
RlA and AfO Register Realien should be unified with Open Refine. The entry names in RlA shoudl be updated to modern standards or our standards e.g. [Proper names](https://github.com/ElectronicBabylonianLiterature/generic-documentation/blob/master/guides/properNames.md "‌")

Then a collection would be created from them.

- “Dictionary” is renamed to “Dictionaries”
- Two new Tabs are created: `Akkadian` (by default, current contents) and `Realia` (new dictionary)
- The new collection (`realia`) has a schema like this:

```
{
  "_id": "string",
  "relatedTerms": [
    "string"
  ],
  "type": [
    "entityType"
  ],
  "afoRegister": [
  # This is very similar to the
  # akkadischeGlossareUndIndices property
  # in the Word schema. Perhaps simply
  # expand that property with crossReference
  # and use it also here. The same component
  # of the Frontend can be used to
  # display it
    {
      "mainWord": "string",
      "note": string,
      "AfO": string,
      "reference": string,
      "crossReference": string,
    }
  ],
  "references": [
    referenceObject
  ],
  "wikidataId": [
    "string"
  ],
  "reallexikon": [
    {
      "id": "string",
      "title": "string",
      "reference": referenceObject,
      "content": "string"
    }
  ]
}
```

- Use the token annotation tool implemented for entity types to allow linking tokes to specific entities.
- The Reallexikon der Assyriologie should be linked to the [online RlA](https://publikationen.badw.de/de/rla/index#~-0a- "‌"). There is a list of them at ebl-api-`data-preparation/references/RlA`. All bibliographical entries already exist in the bibliography database.
- The search shold look for the `id` + `relatedTerm`, using the already existing collation, plus ignoring `”“"ʾ’'`

---

Intro:

The Dictionary of Realia is a reference tool for the material culture, religion, flora and fauna, and other realia of the ancient Near East, from prehistory to the end of the cuneiform cultures around the turn of the Common Era. Its lemma list is drawn primarily from two foundational resources: the _Reallexikon der Assyriologie und Vorderasiatischen Archäologie_ ([RlA]([https://rla.badw.de/das-projekt.html](https://rla.badw.de/das-projekt.html "smartCard-inline"))), the field’s landmark encyclopedia published between 1928 and 2018, and the “Realien” section of the _Archiv für Orientforschung Register_ ([AfO-Register]([https://orientalistik.univie.ac.at/publikationen/afo/register/](https://orientalistik.univie.ac.at/publikationen/afo/register/ "smartCard-inline") )), starting with Volume 25 (1974–1977). In addition, the cuneiform editions in the eBL Library have been annotated according to this list of realia, allowing users to move seamlessly between dictionary entries and the primary textual sources in which these realia appear.

---

---

Intro:

The Dictionary of Realia is a reference tool for the material culture, religion, flora and fauna, and other realia of the ancient Near East, from prehistory to the end of the cuneiform cultures around the turn of the Common Era. Its lemma list is drawn primarily from two foundational resources: the _Reallexikon der Assyriologie und Vorderasiatischen Archäologie_ ([RlA]([https://rla.badw.de/das-projekt.html](https://rla.badw.de/das-projekt.html "smartCard-inline"))), the field’s landmark encyclopedia published between 1928 and 2018, and the “Realien” section of the _Archiv für Orientforschung Register_ ([AfO-Register]([https://orientalistik.univie.ac.at/publikationen/afo/register/](https://orientalistik.univie.ac.at/publikationen/afo/register/ "smartCard-inline") )), starting with Volume 25 (1974–1977). In addition, the cuneiform editions in the eBL Library have been annotated according to this list of realia, allowing users to move seamlessly between dictionary entries and the primary textual sources in which these realia appear.

---

card.name: Realia

old.desc:
- Tokens can be linked to specific entities, stored in a collection.
- As discussed, as possibility would be to introduce all entries in the Reallexikon der Assyriologie as entitities. There is a list of them at ebl-api-`data-preparation/references/RlA`. All bibliographical entries already exist in the bibliography database.
- Schema for the entities:

```
{
"_id" : string,
"type" : entityTypes,
"references" : [ referenceObject ],
"wikidataId" : string,
"reallexikonId" : string
}
```

- Use the token annotation tool implemented for entity types.

card.desc:
RlA and AfO Register Realien should be unified with Open Refine. The entry names in RlA shoudl be updated to modern standards or our standards e.g. [Proper names](https://github.com/ElectronicBabylonianLiterature/generic-documentation/blob/master/guides/properNames.md "‌")

Then a collection would be created from them.

‌

- Tokens can be linked to specific entities, stored in a collection.
- The Reallexikon der Assyriologie should be linked to the [online RlA](https://publikationen.badw.de/de/rla/index#~-0a- "‌"). There is a list of them at ebl-api-`data-preparation/references/RlA`. All bibliographical entries already exist in the bibliography database.
- Schema for the entities:

```
{
"_id" : string,
"type" : entityTypes,
"references" : [ referenceObject ],
"wikidataId" : string,
"reallexikonId" : string
}
```

- Use the token annotation tool implemented for entity types.

card.name: Implement Entity Annotation

data.text:
From another card:

ENTITIES COLLECTION?
ONLY for personal names, and as an **optional** field at this stage (it is completely impossible to search all identifications of persons in the edited Old Babylonian and Ur III texts, and there are so many identical names of different persons):

(8) Prosopography = identification of individuals: 1) provide a tool so that various instances of the same name can be marked as referring to the same person (most important in that regard is the “Dossier” field, and this is why it should be shown first in the references of names); 2) allow “second name” of the same person (including hypocoristica, not infrequent)

(8a) Commentary to individual (in the PNA style, for example)

(8b) Literature on individual

‌

Some remarks:

Transliteration(s); often, various transliterations/spellings are possible for the same name; but this is different from the “second name”-category

(1) to (7) could be the work for Felix in 2025; the prosopography can be left to the editors, i.e. the two Anna’s

A good handling of the “Prosopography” field helps to avoid the strange category “Royal name”; it would be very helpful to lemmatize all names, whether royal or not…

Categories of names: to be discussed (ORACC list to be adjusted, it is not 100% perfect)
